dimension_results.detail: BA's factual claims are well-evidenced. (1) Root cause claim ('task 20260526-052559 cleaned the sentinel writer but not surrounding hooks') is supported by observed grep results showing 0 matches in write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py and positive matches in all 3 hook files. (2) Affected file identification cites exact line ranges (pretool-write-guard.sh:12-29, pretool-bash-safety.sh:633-669/933/939/947/961, stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh:51-61) — QA verified all line ranges match the actual dead-code locations by reading each file. (3) The context JSON records grep output as 'evidence_type: observed'. (4) Codex consultation is documented with 5 findings, dispositions, and rationale for each.

User requirement: 'Remove all dead code referencing auth-flag from 3 hook files'. BA's scope covers exactly these 3 files with no narrowing or renaming. The exclusion list (write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py, commands/commit.md, pretool-git-privilege-guard.py, tests/generated/*) is justified — user explicitly said those are already clean or out of scope. QA independently grepped all .sh and .py files in the repo and confirmed the only .sh files with dead auth-flag references are the 3 BA identified. The 14 .py files with matches are all in tests/generated/ which BA correctly excluded per user instruction. No universal-scope claims in user requirement that BA narrowed improperly.

BA conducted a complete investigation: (1) grepped 'bulk-allowed' across hooks/ to enumerate all affected lines, (2) verified write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py was already clean (0 matches), (3) identified specific code patterns to remove (case block alternatives, grep conditions, comments, reap section), (4) consulted codex which surfaced 2 additional issues BA missed (expanded grep pattern for AC1 to catch 'auth-flag'/'bulk-auth' terminology; stale comment at line 933). Both were incorporated. No investigation shortcuts detected.

QA verified all 3 file paths exist and contain the dead code BA identified: (1) pretool-write-guard.sh lines 12-29 — confirmed: Layer A comment block references 'auth-flag', line 21 case pattern includes '/tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-*.flag', line 22 BLOCKED message references it, line 25 references 'bulk-auth flag'. (2) pretool-bash-safety.sh lines 633-669 — confirmed: Layer 1.F comment block references 'auth-flag' and 'bulk-allowed' extensively, line 669 grep condition for '/tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-'. Lines 933, 939, 947, 961 — confirmed: line 933 comment says 'script-level gate handles auth', lines 939/947 BLOCKED messages use 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write' token, line 961 says 'create the auth flag'. (3) stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh lines 51-61 — confirmed: entire 'Bulk auth-flag reap' section with BULK_AUTH_FLAG variable, if-block, and rm command for a file that is never created.

AC1 check command in ticket and AC JSON are identical: 'grep -rn -E "bulk-allowed|bulk[- ]?auth|auth[- ]flag|bulk-commit-auth-flag" hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh hooks/stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh' with expected exit code 1 and empty stdout. AC2 check command: 'grep -c "claude-bulk-commit-sentinel" hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh' with expected exit code 0 and min count 1 per file. Both commands are directly runnable and produce deterministic pass/fail results. No substitution or drift between ticket prose ACs and structured AC JSON commands. Note: ticket has 3 ACs (AC2 for write-guard, AC3 for bash-safety) while AC JSON merges them into one AC2 covering both files — this is scope-equivalent, not drift.

ac_coverage_check.coverage_note: Ticket AC2+AC3 merged into JSON AC2 (single grep -c covering both files). Functionally equivalent coverage — both files checked for sentinel preservation in one command.

notes: BA codex consultation was productive — expanded AC1 regex and caught a stale comment at bash-safety.sh:933 that BA's initial pass missed. Both incorporated.
The stable stderr token 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write' in BLOCKED messages (lines 939, 947) is correctly flagged for update to 'bulk-commit-sentinel-write' in Technical Hints section.
14 test files in tests/generated/ contain auth-flag references but are correctly excluded per user instruction — these are historical generated tests for prior tasks.
